chapter_title.ar: باب الْوَقْتِ الَّذِي تَبِينُ مِنْهُ الْمُطَلَّقَةُ وَ الَّذِي يَكُونُ فِيهِ الرَّجْعَةُ مَتَى يَجُوزُ لَهَا أَنْ تَتَزَوَّجَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 26 – The time which she becomes irrevocably divorced from him, and in which there happens to be the reversion, when is it allowed for her to re-marry?

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/149)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/149)
text: قُلْتُ لَهُ رَجُلٌ طَلَّقَ امْرَأَتَهُ قَالَ هُوَ أَحَقُّ بِرَجْعَتِهَا مَا لَمْ تَقَعْ فِي الدَّمِ مِنَ الْحَيْضَةِ الثَّالِثَةِ .

en.hubeali: Abu Ali Al Ashary, from Muhammad Bin Abdul Jabbar, from Safwan, from Ishaq Bin Ammar, from Ismail Al Ju’fy,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘A man divorces his wife’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘He is more rightful with reverting back to her for as long as blood has not fallen from the third menstruation’.
en.sarwar: 4. Abu Ali al-Ash‘ariy has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Abd al-Jabbar from Safwan from Ishaq ibn ‘Ammar from ’Isma‘il al-Ju‘fiy who has said the following: “I once said to abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, that a man has divorced his wife. He (the Imam) said, ‘He has the right to go back to her as long as she has not seen blood discharge because of Hayd (menses) for the third time (after divorce).”’
